_doc: Per-bench allowlist for `node scripts/bench.mjs --diagnostic-detail`.
Each top-level key is the basename of a bench target (i.e. the
directory name under `bench/`, e.g. "control-svelte-5"). Each
value is a record of four directional symmetric-diff allowlists:
ours_minus_upstream    — diagnostics ours fires that upstream
(non-tsgo) does not. Usually false
positives we know are wrong.
upstream_minus_ours    — diagnostics upstream fires that ours
misses. Usually rules we haven't ported.
ours_minus_tsgo        — same as ours_minus_upstream, but vs
`svelte-check --tsgo`.
tsgo_minus_ours        — same as upstream_minus_ours, but vs
`svelte-check --tsgo`.
Each entry is the literal '<file>:<line>:<col> [<code>]' string
from the diff output — paste-from-stderr exactly. The runner
warns on stale entries (allowlisted but no longer firing) so
the file decays toward zero as we close gaps. Adding a new
entry requires a one-line justification in this _doc block
below — keep the file living, not stagnant.
Populating this file: run `node scripts/bench.mjs --target
bench/<name> --mode parity --diagnostic-detail` against each
bench, eyeball the symmetric-diff output, and add the entries
you've already triaged as known/expected.
Per-bench notes:
- control-svelte-5: ours emits no svn-lint warnings on this
workspace today (separate investigation). The 49
upstream_minus_ours entries are svn-lint codes ours should
fire too — track via 'fix svn-lint dispatch on this bench',
not by allowlisting them. Left empty deliberately.
